title: adenocarpus hispanicus
intro: Adenocarpus hispanicus, also known as the Spanish broom or retama, is a flowering plant native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Adenocarpus hispanicus include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce swelling, and promote wound healing. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the leaves and stems, then infusing them in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
